## What You Will Learn

**Dropout** is one of the simplest and most effective regularization techniques in deep learning. During training, it randomly zeroes out a fraction of neurons, forcing the network to learn redundant representations.

### Key Concepts
- **nn.Dropout(p)**: Randomly sets elements to zero with probability p during training. At p=0.5, half the neurons are dropped.
- **Regularization**: Prevents overfitting by making the model less dependent on any single neuron. At test time, dropout is automatically disabled.
- **Where to Place Dropout**: Typically after activation layers and before the final classification layer.

### Why This Matters
Dropout was a breakthrough that made deep networks trainable without massive overfitting. It is used in almost every architecture, from CNNs to Transformers.
